{{Quote|You’re appealing to my conscience?|Threkin Horm|<ref name="TG" />}}
'''Threkin Horm''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Before the planet's [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destruction]], his family controlled a spaceline that serviced Alderaan. Because he was grossly overweight, Horm often traveled in a [[Repulsorlift chair/Legends|repulsorlift chair]] in his later [[Standard year/Legends|years]].

==Biography==
During [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Horm served as [[President/Legends|President]] of the [[Alderaanian Council]]. After the [[Battle of New Alderaan (Hunt for Zsinj)|First Battle of New Alderaan]], the Council was dealing with the problem of resettling the refugees of [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]], who were previously refugees from [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. When [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Isolder]] of the [[Hapes Cluster/Legends|Hapes Cluster]] asked fellow [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] to [[Marriage/Legends|marry]] him, Horm believed that her acceptance of the offer could solve the refugee problem. As [[Queen Mother]] of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], Leia could find a suitable planet for the refugees to settle within the star cluster.<ref name="The Courtship of Princess Leia" />

As Horm pressured Leia to accept the offer he earned the lasting enmity of the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], who was also courting Leia. Thus, Horm arranged for Solo to take a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|star destroyer]] to the [[Roche system]], sending him far away from Coruscant so that Leia would forget about him, but Solo kidnapped the Princess in response. This eventually indirectly resulted in the revelation that Horm's mother was the illegitimate daughter of [[Dalla Solo|Dalla Suul]], a notorious [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]]-which, assuming Han Solo was Suul's grandson, would make them half-cousins.<ref name="The Courtship of Princess Leia" />

Horm later attempted to illegally sell the art piece ''[[Killik Twilight]]'' at an auction on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] (in fact it was very likely that he had illegally acquired the moss-painting in the first place). Han and Leia, who had come to the planet to purchase the painting discovered that he was the person who was auctioning it off—with [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] being among the other prospective buyers. Han and Leia blackmailed Horm into not selling the moss-painting but this decision resulted in a melee. During the fight, Horm was hit by a number of toxic darts, which carried a fast-acting neurotoxin that [[Death/Legends|killed]] him.<ref name="TG" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Kevin J. Anderson]] originally intended to include Threkin Horm in his books [[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']] and ''[[Champions of the Force]]'', but a typographical error was committed, resulting in the creation of the character known as [[Hrekin Thorm]]. This may serve as a sort of out-of-universe explanation as to why Thorm's character was in favor of the execution of [[Kyp Durron]] for the destruction of [[Carida/Legends|Carida]]. The later-released novel ''[[Tatooine Ghost]]'' depicts the death of Horm, chronologically prior to Thorm's first appearance.
